description: One devoted to learning and study; a person of deep knowledge. 'Ronua' names who studies deeply—mind given to understanding.
sound_symbolism: The word contemplates deeply. 'Tho' begins with sustained dental awareness, the focused attention of long study. 'Nua' grounds with accumulated presence. A scholar as someone whose awareness has deepened into wisdom.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo thonua' (the scholar). Deeper than 'ronai' (student)—one who has studied long. Related to 'phewo' (sage) as knowledge to wisdom.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Scholar as seeker—devoted to understanding, not attached to being right.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Scholar as keeper—maintaining and transmitting accumulated knowledge.
